TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua sitsuayan a tsautsau se Peɫeti ɫa Dakudakuts namepaɫi.
S.PHON: izua siʦuajan a ʦauʦau sə pəʎəti ʎa dakudakuʦ naməpaʎi.
S.FORM: izua sicuayan a caucau se Peljeti lja Dakudakuc namepalji.
S.PHON: izua siʦuajan a ʦauʦau sə pəʎəti ʎa dakudakuʦ naməpaʎi.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a person from Peɫeti of the Dakudakuts clan who became an evil-eye man.

S.FORM: nu patsun ta lalangaw, ta qatsang, ta kuka, ta tsautsau, matsay sakamaya penuɫat, aya.
S.PHON: nu paʦun ta ɭaɭaŋaw, ta qaʦaŋ, ta kuka, ta ʦauʦau, maʦaj sakamaja pənuʎat, aja.
S.FORM: nu pacun ta lalangaw, ta qacang, ta kuka, ta caucau, macay sakamaya penuljat, aya.
S.PHON: nu paʦun ta ɭaɭaŋaw, ta qaʦaŋ, ta kuka, ta ʦauʦau, maʦaj sakamaja pənuʎat, aja.
S.TRANSL: If he looked at flies, pigs, chicken or people, they all just died, they say.

S.FORM: marekutj a sikataqaɫan, sa kemim ta umaq.
S.PHON: marəkuc a sikataqaʎan, sa kəmim ta umaq.
S.FORM: marekutj a sikataqaljan, sa kemim ta umaq.
S.PHON: marəkuʦ a sikataqaʎan, sa kəmim ta umaq.
S.TRANSL: The villagers were afraid, and looked for somewhere for him to live.

S.FORM: djemumak anga i sadip ta tjaguɫ; saka seman umaq; sa sematjez anga pasa zua a sikataqaɫan.
S.PHON: ɉəmumak aŋa i sadip ta caguʎ; saka səman umaq; sa səmacəz aŋa pasa zua a sikataqaʎan.
S.FORM: djemumak anga i sadip ta tjagulj; saka seman umaq; sa sematjez anga pasa zua a sikataqaljan.
S.PHON: ɉəmumak aŋa i sadip ta ʦaguʎ; saka səman umaq; sa səmaʦəz aŋa pasa zua a sikataqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: They found a rock-face in the foothills and made a house there; and the villagers sent him there.

S.FORM: saka pavaivai anga sakamaya senemanemanga, patje se Tenatseng a qinaɫan a pairang.
S.PHON: saka pavaivai aŋa sakamaja sənəmanəmaŋa, pacə sə tənaʦəŋ a qinaʎan a pairaŋ.
S.FORM: saka pavaivai anga sakamaya senemanemanga, patje se Tenaceng a qinaljan a pairang.
S.PHON: saka pavaivai aŋa sakamaja sənəmanəmaŋa, paʦə sə tənaʦəŋ a qinaʎan a pairaŋ.
S.TRANSL: People from everywhere gave him food, including the plainspeople in the village of Tenatseng.

S.FORM: saka masutan aravats a senemanemanga.
S.PHON: saka masutan aravaʦ a sənəmanəmaŋa.
S.FORM: saka masutan aravac a senemanemanga.
S.PHON: saka masutan aravaʦ a sənəmanəmaŋa.
S.TRANSL: So everyone lost heavily,

S.FORM: saka limutseng a pairang, sa "ari, qemtsii," aya maɫavar tia pairang.
S.PHON: saka ɭimuʦəŋ a pairaŋ, sa "ari, qəmʦii," aja maʎavar tia pairaŋ.
S.FORM: saka limuceng a pairang, sa "ari, qemcii," aya maljavar tia pairang.
S.PHON: saka ɭimuʦəŋ a pairaŋ, sa "ari, qəmʦii," aja maʎavar tia pairaŋ.
S.TRANSL: and the plainspeople got angry, and decided: “Come on, let’s kill him.”

S.FORM: vaik a sema zua i ta umaq ni paɫi, sa djemaɫun anga.
S.PHON: vaik a səma zua i ta umaq ni paʎi, sa ɉəmaʎun aŋa.
S.FORM: vaik a sema zua i ta umaq ni palji, sa djemaljun anga.
S.PHON: vaik a səma zua i ta umaq ni paʎi, sa ɉəmaʎun aŋa.
S.TRANSL: They went to the evil-eye man’s house. When they got there,

S.FORM: "kiqiɫau.
S.PHON: "kiqiʎau.
S.FORM: "kiqiljau.
S.PHON: "kiqiʎau.
S.TRANSL: they said: “Hide yourself.

S.FORM: mangtjez amen a pavay ta lami."
S.PHON: maŋcəz amən a pavaj ta ɭami."
S.FORM: mangtjez amen a pavay ta lami."
S.PHON: maŋʦəz amən a pavaj ta ɭami."
S.TRANSL: We’ve come to give you food.”

S.FORM: sa kembung timadju a paɫi; sa qetsin na pairang azua timadju a paɫi.
S.PHON: sa kəmbuŋ timaɉu a paʎi; sa qəʦin na pairaŋ azua timaɉu a paʎi.
S.FORM: sa kembung timadju a palji; sa qecin na pairang azua timadju a palji.
S.PHON: sa kəmbuŋ timaɉu a paʎi; sa qəʦin na pairaŋ azua timaɉu a paʎi.
S.TRANSL: So the evil-eye man bent down, and the plainspeople killed him.

S.FORM: manu mitsetsikal ti paɫi; pavalet a patsun.
S.PHON: manu miʦəʦikaɭ ti paʎi; pavaɭət a paʦun.
S.FORM: manu micecikal ti palji; pavalet a pacun.
S.PHON: manu miʦəʦikaɭ ti paʎi; pavaɭət a paʦun.
S.TRANSL: But the evil-eye man became violent and looked at them in revenge;

S.FORM: saka matsay anga sakamaya a pairang.
S.PHON: saka maʦaj aŋa sakamaja a pairaŋ.
S.FORM: saka macay anga sakamaya a pairang.
S.PHON: saka maʦaj aŋa sakamaja a pairaŋ.
S.TRANSL: and the plainspeople just died.

S.FORM: mapuɫu anga a tjeza a matsay.
S.PHON: mapuʎu aŋa a cəza a maʦaj.
S.FORM: mapulju anga a tjeza a macay.
S.PHON: mapuʎu aŋa a ʦəza a maʦaj.
S.TRANSL: Only a few survived and didn’t die.

S.FORM: saka matsay ti paɫi, sa kiquluan, sa tsavuin ta sitsavuan, sa kematsu sema umaq.
S.PHON: saka maʦaj ti paʎi, sa kiquɭuan, sa ʦavuin ta siʦavuan, sa kəmaʦu səma umaq.
S.FORM: saka macay ti palji, sa kiquluan, sa cavuin ta sicavuan, sa kemacu sema umaq.
S.PHON: saka maʦaj ti paʎi, sa kiquɭuan, sa ʦavuin ta siʦavuan, sa kəmaʦu səma umaq.
S.TRANSL: So the evil-eye man died, and they cut off his head, and wrapped it in a blanket; and brought it home.

S.FORM: sa mangtjez i umaq, saka semutsavu.
S.PHON: sa maŋcəz i umaq, saka səmuʦavu.
S.FORM: sa mangtjez i umaq, saka semucavu.
S.PHON: sa maŋʦəz i umaq, saka səmuʦavu.
S.TRANSL: When they got home, they unwrapped it.

S.FORM: tjuruvu a ɫemizaw a qaqunuan vavayavayan.
S.PHON: curuvu a ʎəmizaw a qaqunuan vavajavajan.
S.FORM: tjuruvu a ljemizaw a qaqunuan vavayavayan.
S.PHON: ʦuruvu a ʎəmizaw a qaqunuan vavajavajan.
S.TRANSL: There were many children and women watching.

S.FORM: manu patsun ta matsa, kimatsay anga a mapuɫat.
S.PHON: manu paʦun ta maʦa, kimaʦaj aŋa a mapuʎat.
S.FORM: manu pacun ta maca, kimacay anga a mapuljat.
S.PHON: manu paʦun ta maʦa, kimaʦaj aŋa a mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: And by looking at the evil-eye man’s eyes they all killed themselves.

S.FORM: sa "nakuya," aya azua tjeza a matsay; sa sematjez pasa pana i taladj ta vatsaɫ.
S.PHON: sa "nakuja," aja azua cəza a maʦaj; sa səmacəz pasa pana i taɭaɉ ta vaʦaʎ.
S.FORM: sa "nakuya," aya azua tjeza a macay; sa sematjez pasa pana i taladj ta vacalj.
S.PHON: sa "nakuja," aja azua ʦəza a maʦaj; sa səmaʦəz pasa pana i taɭaɉ ta vaʦaʎ.
S.TRANSL: “That’s bad,” said those who survived and didn’t die. So they took the head to the river to a deep pool.

S.FORM: saka palisi anga palalaut.
S.PHON: saka paɭisi aŋa paɭaɭaut.
S.FORM: saka palisi anga palalaut.
S.PHON: saka paɭisi aŋa paɭaɭaut.
S.TRANSL: Ever since it is taboo to go there.

S.FORM: nu sema zua, kisaqetju anga sakamaya, aya.
S.PHON: nu səma zua, kisaqəcu aŋa sakamaja, aja.
S.FORM: nu sema zua, kisaqetju anga sakamaya, aya.
S.PHON: nu səma zua, kisaqəʦu aŋa sakamaja, aja.
S.TRANSL: They say that those who go there bring illness on themselves.

S.FORM: amin.
S.PHON: amin.
S.FORM: amin.
S.PHON: amin.
S.TRANSL: The end.
